name: RequestBin
skip_reason: requestbin.com now issues a permanent redirect to pipedream.com/requestbin. The original standalone RequestBin service was shut down and its functionality absorbed into Pipedream's product, which requires a Pipedream account/workflow to inspect a webhook. There is no independent RequestBin site, pricing, or feature set left to enrich verifiably.

enrichment.short_description: Mockoon is a free, open-source desktop app for designing and running mock REST APIs locally, with an optional paid Cloud tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mockoon: free open-source tool to design and run mock REST APIs locally, with an optional Cloud plan for team collaboration.

features.title: API Mocking
features.description: Design and run mock REST APIs in seconds through an intuitive desktop interface, with no remote deployment or account needed.

features.title: Dynamic Templating & Rules Matching
features.description: Simulate realistic scenarios and test edge cases using templating and rule-based response matching.

features.title: Requests Recording
features.description: Capture real HTTP requests from a live API and replay them to quickly build mocks.

features.title: Proxy Mode
features.description: Forward unmocked requests through to a real API while mocking only selected endpoints.

features.title: OpenAPI Compatibility
features.description: Import and export OpenAPI specifications to fit existing API design workflows.

features.title: CLI & Docker
features.description: Run mocks headlessly on servers, in Docker containers, or serverless platforms (AWS Lambda, GCP Functions, Firebase Functions), and in CI/CD (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, TravisCI).

features.title: Mockoon Cloud Collaboration
features.description: Deploy mocks to the cloud and collaborate with a team in real time (paid Cloud tier).

pros: Free, open-source desktop app with no account required
Supports Windows, macOS, and Linux
CLI and Docker support for CI/CD and headless environments
OpenAPI import/export fits existing API design workflows
Active, offline-first, privacy-friendly project maintained since 2017
cons: Real-time team collaboration and cloud deployment require a paid Mockoon Cloud subscription
Team plan is priced at $100/month billed annually rather than offering a lower-cost individual paid tier
Enterprise pricing is custom/quote-only, requiring direct contact

faqs.question: Is Mockoon free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the desktop application, CLI, and Docker image are open-source and free to use with no account required.

faqs.question: What is Mockoon Cloud?
faqs.answer: A paid add-on offering cloud-hosted mock deployments and real-time team collaboration, with the Team plan starting at $100/month billed annually.

faqs.question: Does Mockoon Cloud offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available (no card needed for work emails; 7 days with a card for others).

faqs.question: What platforms does Mockoon support?
faqs.answer: Windows, macOS, and Linux desktop installs, plus CLI and Docker for servers and CI/CD.

faqs.question: Can Mockoon import OpenAPI specs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mockoon is compatible with OpenAPI for importing and exporting API designs.

use_cases.title: Local API mocking during frontend development
use_cases.description: Frontend and full-stack developers mock backend REST APIs locally before real endpoints exist.

use_cases.title: CI/CD test mocking
use_cases.description: Teams run Mockoon via CLI or Docker in pipelines to mock dependencies for automated tests.

use_cases.title: Team collaboration on shared mocks
use_cases.description: Organizations use Mockoon Cloud to deploy and collaborate on mock APIs in real time.

features: Free desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux
CLI and Docker image for headless/self-hosted use
Full core mocking, templating, and proxy features
3 API mocks deployed in the cloud with real-time collaboration
200 AI-generated endpoints per month per user
Enterprise support with a customer success manager
Custom payment options (PO, invoicing, bank transfer)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mockoon?
paragraphs: Mockoon is an open-source tool for designing and running mock REST APIs. Its free desktop application lets developers create functioning mock APIs in seconds, entirely offline and without creating an account.

Beyond the desktop app, Mockoon offers a CLI and Docker image for running mocks on servers, in CI/CD pipelines, or on serverless platforms, plus an optional paid Mockoon Cloud tier for deploying mocks to the cloud and collaborating with a team in real time.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mockoon is for
paragraphs: Mockoon suits individual developers who want a fast, free, offline way to mock REST APIs during local development, as well as teams that need shared, cloud-hosted mocks and are willing to pay for the Mockoon Cloud Team or Enterprise plan.

enrichment.short_description: MockServer is a free, open-source tool to mock, proxy, and verify HTTP, HTTPS, gRPC, and GraphQL APIs for testing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free open-source MockServer mocks, proxies, and verifies HTTP/HTTPS, gRPC, and GraphQL APIs for fast, deterministic tests.

features.title: Mocking
features.description: Return controllable, dynamic responses for any request to isolate services under test.

features.title: Proxying
features.description: Record, replay, and inject chaos into real traffic passing through a proxy.

features.title: Verification
features.description: Assert which requests were sent, using MockServer as part of test assertions.

features.title: Chaos Testing
features.description: Inject latency, errors, dropped connections, and malformed responses to test resilience.

features.title: Broad Protocol Support
features.description: Supports HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, HTTPS, gRPC, AsyncAPI messaging, GraphQL, and OAuth2/OIDC.

features.title: LLM Mocking
features.description: Mocks OpenAI, Anthropic, and Bedrock-style APIs, including token streaming.

features.title: Interactive Debugging
features.description: Set breakpoints to pause and modify requests and responses live.

features.title: Load Testing
features.description: Run built-in declarative load scenarios.

pros: Completely free and open source, no SaaS account required
Broad protocol support including HTTP/2, HTTP/3, gRPC, and GraphQL
Multiple deployment options: Docker, executable JAR, Helm/Kubernetes, Testcontainers, Homebrew
Client libraries for many languages: Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go, .NET, Rust, PHP
Built-in chaos and load testing in addition to basic mocking
cons: No managed/hosted SaaS offering documented on the site — requires running and managing your own instance
No listed company, founding year, or dedicated commercial support plans
Documentation does not state an explicit license type

faqs.question: Is MockServer free?
faqs.answer: Yes, MockServer is free and open-source with no SaaS account required.

faqs.question: How do I run MockServer?
faqs.answer: Via Docker, an executable JAR, Homebrew, Kubernetes Helm charts, or Testcontainers.

faqs.question: What protocols does MockServer support?
faqs.answer: HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, HTTPS, gRPC, AsyncAPI messaging, GraphQL, and OAuth2/OIDC.

faqs.question: Can MockServer mock AI/LLM APIs?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes built-in mocking for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Bedrock-style APIs with token streaming.

faqs.question: Which languages have client libraries?
faqs.answer: Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go, .NET, Rust, and PHP, plus a REST API and CLI.

use_cases.title: Automated integration testing
use_cases.description: QA teams mock external dependencies to make integration tests fast, isolated, and deterministic.

use_cases.title: Chaos and resilience testing
use_cases.description: Teams inject latency, errors, and malformed responses to test how services handle failures.

use_cases.title: AI application testing
use_cases.description: Developers mock LLM provider APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock) to test AI features without calling live APIs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MockServer?
paragraphs: MockServer is a free, open-source tool that mocks, records, and proxies HTTP and HTTPS services so tests can run fast, isolated, and deterministic.

It supports a broad range of protocols including HTTP/2, HTTP/3, gRPC, GraphQL, AsyncAPI messaging, and OAuth2/OIDC, and can be deployed via Docker, an executable JAR, Kubernetes Helm charts, or Testcontainers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MockServer is for
paragraphs: MockServer is aimed at backend, QA, and SRE engineers who need to mock, verify, and stress-test HTTP-based service dependencies, including teams building and testing AI applications that call LLM provider APIs.

enrichment.short_description: WireMock is an open-source HTTP API simulation tool, with an optional hosted WireMock Cloud tier for teams and enterprises.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: WireMock: open-source API mocking and simulation tool, with WireMock Cloud offering free and enterprise hosted plans.

features.title: Request matching
features.description: Matches requests on URL patterns, headers, query parameters, cookies, and request body.

features.title: Dynamic response templating
features.description: Generates dynamic responses using Handlebars templating.

features.title: Record and playback
features.description: Records real API traffic and replays it for realistic test scenarios.

features.title: Fault simulation
features.description: Simulates network delays and malformed responses to test edge cases.

features.title: Request verification
features.description: Verifies that expected requests were made during test runs.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Runs as a standalone JAR, embedded in tests, or as a Docker container.

features.title: WireMock Cloud (hosted)
features.description: A commercial hosted platform adding stateful mocking, database import, OpenAPI sync, and UI-based mock creation with AI support.

pros: Free and open source with no cost for the core simulation engine
Supports fault simulation, request verification, and Handlebars-based dynamic responses
Flexible deployment as a standalone JAR, embedded library, or Docker container
Broad framework support including Java (Maven, Gradle, Kotlin, Scala SBT) and .NET via WireMock.Net
WireMock Cloud offers a free plan with no credit card required
cons: WireMock Cloud's free plan is limited to 1,000 API calls/month, one user, and 3 mock APIs
WireMock Cloud Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Stateful mocking, team collaboration, and SSO/RBAC require the paid Enterprise tier
Free plan on WireMock Cloud caps request rate at 10 requests per second

faqs.question: Is WireMock free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core WireMock tool is free and open source. WireMock Cloud, the hosted commercial platform, also has a free plan with usage limits.

faqs.question: Does WireMock have a hosted cloud offering?
faqs.answer: Yes, WireMock Cloud is a commercial hosted platform with a free tier (1,000 calls/month, 1 user, 3 mock APIs) and a custom-priced Enterprise tier.

faqs.question: What languages and frameworks does WireMock support?
faqs.answer: WireMock supports Java (via Maven, Gradle for Groovy/Kotlin) and Scala SBT, plus .NET via the WireMock.Net implementation, and can run standalone or in Docker.

faqs.question: Does WireMock Cloud include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, WireMock Cloud offers UI-based mock creation with AI support.

use_cases.title: API testing without live dependencies
use_cases.description: Simulate third-party or internal APIs so applications can be tested without relying on real, live services.

use_cases.title: Parallel feature development
use_cases.description: Develop and test against simulated API responses before the real backend is ready.

use_cases.title: Fault and chaos testing
use_cases.description: Use WireMock Cloud's chaos testing to simulate network delays and malformed responses for resilience testing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is WireMock?
paragraphs: WireMock is an open-source tool for simulating HTTP-based APIs, described by its maker as "the industry standard for scalable API simulation." It lets developers mock request/response behavior, including request matching on URLs, headers, query parameters, cookies, and body content, along with dynamic response generation via Handlebars templating.

Beyond the free, open-source core (which can run standalone, embedded in tests, or in Docker), WireMock also offers WireMock Cloud, a hosted commercial platform with stateful mocking, OpenAPI synchronization, CI/CD integration, and chaos testing. WireMock Cloud has a free tier and a custom-priced Enterprise tier for larger teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who WireMock is for
paragraphs: WireMock suits developers and QA teams working in Java or .NET ecosystems who need to test applications against simulated dependencies, verify request behavior, or develop features in parallel with backend teams before real APIs are available.

enrichment.short_description: Bruno is an open-source, Git-native API client with local-first storage, offering paid Pro and Ultimate plans for teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bruno: open-source, offline-first API client storing collections as Git-tracked files; paid Pro and Ultimate plans add team features.

features.title: Git-native storage
features.description: Collections are stored as plain text files and folders directly in your filesystem, trackable in Git.

features.title: Local-first, no cloud sync
features.description: Data stays on the user's machine with no required cloud synchronization.

features.title: No login required
features.description: The app can be used without creating an account or logging in.

features.title: Version control integration
features.description: Works natively with Git and other version control systems for team collaboration.

features.title: Enterprise permissions via Git
features.description: Leverages existing Git-based access controls and audit logs instead of a proprietary permissions system.

features.title: Cross-platform desktop app and CLI
features.description: Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, plus a CLI for automation.

pros: Fully open source with local-first, offline storage of collections as plain text files
Git-native workflow enables version control and collaboration without a proprietary cloud
No login or account required to use the core application
SOC 2 Type I certified, with no AI training on user data
Cross-platform desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux, plus a CLI
14-day free trial of the Ultimate plan with no credit card required
cons: Free Open Source tier is limited to 2 workspaces, public Git providers only, and 5 OpenAPI syncs per month
SSO/SCIM and enterprise support are only available on the paid Ultimate tier
Unlimited workspaces and private Git provider support require the paid Pro or Ultimate plans
No mobile app is documented

faqs.question: Is Bruno free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bruno's Open Source tier is free ($0). Paid Pro ($6/user/month) and Ultimate ($11/user/month) tiers, billed annually, add team and enterprise features.

faqs.question: Does Bruno store data in the cloud?
faqs.answer: No, Bruno is local-first: collections are stored as plain text files on your machine, with sharing handled through Git.

faqs.question: What platforms does Bruno support?
faqs.answer: Bruno is available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, plus a CLI, and can be installed via Winget, Chocolatey, or Scoop on Windows.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for the paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial of the Ultimate plan is available without requiring a credit card.

use_cases.title: Offline, Git-based API testing
use_cases.description: Work with a local-first API client that stores collections as files without relying on cloud sync.

use_cases.title: Team API collaboration via Git
use_cases.description: Use the Pro or Ultimate plan to share collections across a team using Git-based access controls.

use_cases.title: Enterprise API governance
use_cases.description: Use the Ultimate plan's SSO/SCIM, custom terms, and security review for organization-wide rollout.

features: Custom terms, DPA, and security review

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bruno?
paragraphs: Bruno is an open-source API client positioned as a developer-first alternative to cloud-based API platforms. It stores collections as plain text files directly in the filesystem, so they can be tracked and shared through Git rather than a proprietary cloud sync layer.

Beyond the free Open Source tier, Bruno offers paid Pro ($6/user/month) and Ultimate ($11/user/month) plans, billed annually, that add unlimited workspaces, private Git provider support, unlimited OpenAPI syncs, and, on Ultimate, SSO/SCIM and enterprise support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bruno is for
paragraphs: Bruno is aimed at developers and teams who want a local-first, Git-native API client without mandatory cloud accounts, and at organizations (including Microsoft, Autodesk, Capital One, Cisco, Comcast, GitHub, Oracle, Nike, and Vanguard, per the site) that want Git-based governance over API collections.

enrichment.short_description: Insomnia is a Kong-owned API platform supporting REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and WebSocket, with free and paid plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Insomnia by Kong: API client for REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and WebSocket testing, with a free Essentials tier and paid Pro/Enterprise plans.

features.title: Multi-protocol API client
features.description: Supports REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket (WSS), and Socket.io endpoints.

features.title: API design
features.description: OpenAPI spec-based design with real-time preview.

features.title: API testing
features.description: Unlimited collection tests with pre/post-request scripting.

features.title: API mocking
features.description: Generates dynamic mock responses from specs or descriptions.

features.title: MCP client
features.description: Native Model Context Protocol server testing support.

features.title: Inso CLI
features.description: Command-line tool for CI/CD automation and spec validation.

features.title: Git sync collaboration
features.description: Syncs projects via Git, local storage, or cloud, with role-based access controls.

pros: Free Essentials tier includes unlimited projects, collection runner, and full HTTP/gRPC/GraphQL support
Supports REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket (WSS), and Socket.io protocols in one client
Includes Inso CLI for CI/CD automation and spec validation
Native MCP (Model Context Protocol) client for testing MCP servers
Enterprise plan adds SSO/SCIM, native vault integrations (AWS, GCP, HashiCorp, Azure), and SOC 2 Type 2 compliance
cons: Free Essentials tier limits Git Sync to 3 users and mock server requests to 1,000/month
SSO (SAML/OIDC) is only available on the $45/user/month Enterprise plan
Enterprise self-serve pricing caps at 50 users; larger teams must contact sales

faqs.question: Is Insomnia free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Essentials plan is free ($0/user/month). Paid Pro ($12/user/month) and Enterprise ($45/user/month) plans, billed annually, add collaboration and governance features.

faqs.question: Who owns Insomnia?
faqs.answer: Insomnia is owned by Kong Inc.

faqs.question: What API protocols does Insomnia support?
faqs.answer: REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket (WSS), and Socket.io.

faqs.question: Does Insomnia offer a CLI?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Inso CLI supports CI/CD automation and API spec validation.

faqs.question: Does Insomnia support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, SSO via SAML/OIDC with SCIM automation is available on the Enterprise plan.

use_cases.title: API design and testing
use_cases.description: Design, test, and debug REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and WebSocket APIs using the free Essentials tier.

use_cases.title: Team collaboration on APIs
use_cases.description: Use the Pro plan for unlimited Git Sync users, organizations, and 10,000 monthly mock server requests.

use_cases.title: Enterprise API governance
use_cases.description: Use the Enterprise plan for SSO, native vault integrations, domain capture, and dedicated support.

features: Git Sync for up to 3 users
No login required for Scratch Pad
Full HTTP, gRPC, and GraphQL support
Native vault integrations (AWS, GCP, HashiCorp, Azure)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Insomnia?
paragraphs: Insomnia is an API development platform owned by Kong Inc., used for designing, testing, debugging, and mocking APIs across REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket (WSS), and Socket.io protocols. It includes the Inso CLI for CI/CD automation and a native MCP client for testing Model Context Protocol servers.

Insomnia offers a free Essentials tier alongside paid Pro ($12/user/month) and Enterprise ($45/user/month) plans, billed annually, which add unlimited Git Sync collaborators, higher mock server request limits, SSO/SCIM, and native secrets-vault integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Insomnia is for
paragraphs: Insomnia is used by individual developers on its free Essentials tier, scaling teams on the Pro plan needing broader Git Sync collaboration, and large organizations on the Enterprise plan requiring SSO, vault integrations, and dedicated support.

enrichment.short_description: HTTPie is an open-source API testing toolset with a CLI, a cross-platform desktop app, and a web app.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HTTPie: open-source CLI, desktop, and web API client with AI-assisted request building. See features, platforms, and what's verified.

features.title: Terminal/CLI
features.description: The original open-source command-line tool, installable via apt, brew, choco, pip, port, snap, yum, and more; installs both `http` and `https` commands.

features.title: Expressive syntax
features.description: Expressive and intuitive command syntax with first-class JSON support and colorized, formatted terminal output.

features.title: Desktop app
features.description: A graphical, cross-platform desktop application (Mac, Windows, Linux) currently in public beta, offering the CLI's functionality in a GUI.

features.title: Web app
features.description: A browser-based version of HTTPie accessible without installing anything.

features.title: AI-assisted work
features.description: The Desktop app advertises the ability to 'leverage the power of AI-assisted work' alongside a dedicated HTTPie AI product.

features.title: Organization tools
features.description: Spaces, collections, and tabs for organizing requests, plus variables and environments for request parametrization.

features.title: Offline & account-optional
features.description: The Desktop app can be used offline and without creating an account, with cross-device sync and auto-save when signed in.

features.title: Sessions and auth
features.description: Persistent sessions, forms and file uploads, HTTPS/proxy/authentication support, a wget-like download mode, and an extensions API for authentication.

pros: CLI is free, open source, and has over 47,900 GitHub stars
Available across CLI, desktop, and web, so teams can pick the interface that fits their workflow
Desktop app works offline and doesn't require an account
Simple, human-readable request syntax with strong JSON support
Backed by $6.5M seed funding from Coatue, Blossom Capital, and founders of Intercom, Checkout.com, and Monzo
cons: The Desktop app is still in public beta
No public pricing page was found, so cost of any paid tiers (if they exist) is unclear
Detailed documentation on integrations and enterprise/team features is not surfaced on the marketing site

faqs.question: Is HTTPie free?
faqs.answer: The HTTPie CLI is free and open source. The Desktop app can also be used offline and without an account; no public pricing page currently lists paid plans.

faqs.question: What platforms does HTTPie Desktop support?
faqs.answer: HTTPie Desktop is described as cross-platform, supporting Mac, Windows, and Linux.

faqs.question: Is HTTPie CLI open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the CLI is open source, with the project describing itself as 'Open Source. Open minded' and having over 47,900 GitHub stars.

faqs.question: Does HTTPie have AI features?
faqs.answer: HTTPie markets 'AI-assisted work' in its Desktop app and has a separate HTTPie AI product, though detailed capabilities are not fully documented on the homepage.

use_cases.title: Command-line API testing
use_cases.description: Developers use the HTTPie CLI to send and debug HTTP requests directly from the terminal with readable, colorized output.

use_cases.title: GUI-based API workflows
use_cases.description: Teams that prefer a visual interface can use the Desktop or Web app to organize requests into spaces and collections.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HTTPie?
paragraphs: HTTPie is an API testing toolset that started as an open-source command-line tool and has grown into a suite including a cross-platform Desktop app (public beta) and a Web app. The CLI supports expressive syntax, first-class JSON handling, and colorized terminal output, and is installable through common package managers like apt, brew, pip, and snap.
The company, HTTPie Inc., is based in San Francisco and has raised a $6.5M seed round from investors including Coatue and Blossom Capital. The CLI alone has amassed over 47,900 GitHub stars.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HTTPie is for
paragraphs: HTTPie suits developers who want a fast, readable way to test APIs from the terminal, as well as teams who prefer a GUI via the Desktop or Web app for organizing requests into collections and spaces.

enrichment.short_description: Yaak is a local-first, MIT-licensed desktop API client for HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE requests.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Yaak: open-source, local-first API client for HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket and SSE. Free for personal use; paid licenses for commercial use.

features.title: Multi-protocol requests
features.description: Send HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE requests from one app.

features.title: Dynamic templating
features.description: Request templating with variables and template functions.

features.title: Full request debugging
features.description: Inspect redirects, cookies, and headers for each request.

features.title: Built-in Git collaboration
features.description: Projects are stored as plain-text files with built-in Git integration for team collaboration.

features.title: Import from other tools
features.description: Import existing collections from Postman, Insomnia, OpenAPI, cURL, and Swagger.

features.title: Broad authentication support
features.description: Automatically handles OAuth 1/2, AWS v4, JWT, Basic, Bearer, and API Key authentication.

features.title: Local-only privacy
features.description: Local-only data storage, secrets encrypted with ChaCha20-Poly1305 in the OS keychain, and zero telemetry.

features.title: Agent/MCP connectivity
features.description: Connect your own AI agents via the CLI or an MCP server.

pros: MIT-licensed source code that can be audited and self-built
Free for personal use with no account required
Local-first with zero telemetry and encrypted secret storage
Supports HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE in one app
Built-in Git collaboration using plain-text project files
cons: Prebuilt binaries require a paid commercial license for business use even though the source is MIT-licensed
Desktop-only (Mac, Windows, Linux) — no web or mobile version
No native AI features; AI usage requires connecting your own agents

faqs.question: Is Yaak free?
faqs.answer: Yaak is free for personal use. Commercial use of the prebuilt binaries requires a paid license (annual or lifetime), even though the source code is MIT-licensed.

faqs.question: Is Yaak open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the source code is MIT-licensed and can be audited or self-built; licensing fees apply only to the official prebuilt binaries.

faqs.question: What platforms does Yaak support?
faqs.answer: Yaak is available for Mac, Windows, and Linux.

faqs.question: Does Yaak have built-in AI?
faqs.answer: No native AI is built in, but Yaak lets you connect your own agents via its CLI or MCP server.

faqs.question: Who makes Yaak?
faqs.answer: Yaak is made by Mountain Loop Labs, founded by Greg, the creator of Insomnia (sold in 2019). Yaak launched in January 2024.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused API testing
use_cases.description: Developers who want a local-first client with zero telemetry and encrypted secrets can test APIs without sending data to a cloud service.

use_cases.title: Multi-protocol development
use_cases.description: Teams working across REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and WebSocket APIs can use a single client instead of separate tools.

use_cases.title: Team collaboration via Git
use_cases.description: Since projects are stored as plain-text files, teams can collaborate on API collections using their existing Git workflow.

features: 30-day free trial for commercial evaluation, no credit card required

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Yaak?
paragraphs: Yaak is a local-first desktop API client for sending HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE requests. It's built by Mountain Loop Labs, founded by Greg, the creator of Insomnia (sold in 2019), and launched in January 2024.

The source code is MIT-licensed and can be audited or self-built, but the official prebuilt binaries require a paid commercial license for business use — the app is free only for personal use, with a 30-day trial for commercial evaluation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Yaak is for
paragraphs: Yaak fits developers who want a privacy-respecting, local-first API client for multiple protocols, and teams that prefer storing API collections as plain-text files for Git-based collaboration.

enrichment.short_description: Scalar builds API interfaces: hosted docs, an open-source API client, SDK generation, and an API registry.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scalar: hosted API docs, open-source API client, SDK generation, and API registry. Free plan available; Pro starts at $72/month.

features.title: API Docs
features.description: Write documentation using Markdown, MDX, OpenAPI, and AsyncAPI, with two-way Git synchronization and support for custom HTML/CSS/JS.

features.title: AI/MCP chat
features.description: API Docs include AI/MCP chat capabilities for interacting with documentation.

features.title: SDK generation
features.description: Generates type-safe client libraries from OpenAPI documents in TypeScript, Python, Golang, PHP, Java, and Ruby.

features.title: API Client
features.description: An open-source, offline-first, cross-platform (Linux, Windows, macOS) API client built on OpenAPI standards, positioned as a Postman alternative.

features.title: API Registry
features.description: Manages and versions OpenAPI documents with Git integration.

features.title: Hosted MCP servers
features.description: Pro plan includes hosted MCP servers and automated GitHub workflows.

features.title: Custom domains
features.description: Pro plan supports custom domains and subdomains for hosted documentation.

pros: Free plan includes hosted OpenAPI docs, a built-in API client, and unlimited viewer seats
API Client is open-source and offline-first with no vendor lock-in
SDK generation covers six languages (TypeScript, Python, Golang, PHP, Java, Ruby)
Two-way Git synchronization for documentation content
Enterprise plan offers SSO/SAML, RBAC, and SLA-backed priority support
cons: Pro plan is $72/month plus $100/month per additional individual SDK language
Free plan is limited to 1 editor seat and 50 Agent Scalar credits
Enterprise pricing requires booking a demo rather than public quoting

faqs.question: Is Scalar free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scalar offers a Free plan with hosted OpenAPI docs, a built-in API client, 50 Agent Scalar credits, unlimited viewer seats, and 1 editor seat.

faqs.question: How much does Scalar Pro cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan is $72/month and adds custom domains, Git Sync with Markdown/MDX, SDKs, hosted MCP servers, automated GitHub workflows, and more editor seats.

faqs.question: Is the Scalar API Client open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scalar's API Client is open-source, offline-first, and cross-platform (Linux, Windows, macOS).

faqs.question: What languages does Scalar generate SDKs for?
faqs.answer: Scalar can generate type-safe SDKs in TypeScript, Python, Golang, PHP, Java, and Ruby; each additional individual SDK language costs $100/month on Pro or Enterprise.

faqs.question: Does Scalar have Enterprise features?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan (custom pricing) includes full developer portals, SSO/SAML, RBAC, priority SLA support, and SDK/MCP migration services.

use_cases.title: Hosted API documentation
use_cases.description: Teams publish OpenAPI-based reference docs with Git-synced Markdown/MDX content and custom branding.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted API testing
use_cases.description: Developers use Scalar's open-source, offline-first API Client as a Postman alternative without vendor lock-in.

use_cases.title: SDK distribution
use_cases.description: Companies generate and maintain type-safe client SDKs across six languages directly from their OpenAPI spec.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Scalar?
paragraphs: Scalar is a platform for building API interfaces: hosted documentation (Markdown, MDX, OpenAPI, AsyncAPI with two-way Git sync), an open-source offline-first API Client positioned as a Postman alternative, SDK generation for six languages, and an API Registry for versioning OpenAPI documents.
The company is led by CEO Marc Laventure and describes its core values as Accessibility, Open-Source, and API First.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Scalar is for
paragraphs: Scalar suits API teams that want hosted, Git-synced documentation plus SDK generation, and individual developers who want an open-source, self-hostable API client as an alternative to closed-source tools.

enrichment.short_description: Stoplight is a design-first platform for building, documenting, mocking, and governing OpenAPI-based APIs, now operating under SmartBear.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Stoplight: design-first API platform for OpenAPI editing, mock servers, docs, and governance, now part of SmartBear.

features.title: Visual API Editor
features.description: A visual editor with comprehensive OpenAPI and JSON Schema support lets teams design and prototype APIs without writing complex syntax by hand.

features.title: Automatic Mock Servers
features.description: Mock servers are generated automatically, stay up-to-date with the API design, and support request and response validation for testing.

features.title: Interactive Documentation
features.description: API documentation is generated automatically alongside design work, with a live preview available directly in the editor.

features.title: Style Guides & Governance
features.description: Built-in and custom style guides enforce design consistency, with shared style guides available on Pro and Enterprise plans to promote governance across an API portfolio.

features.title: Component Reusability
features.description: Schemas and components can be reused across multiple APIs, with centralized updates that propagate to all dependent APIs.

features.title: Git Sync
features.description: Integrates with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure so teams can work with API design artifacts directly in their existing Git workflow.

features.title: SSO & Activity Logs
features.description: The Pro Team plan adds LDAP/SAML SSO, workspace groups, and activity logs for larger organizations.

features.title: Open Source Tooling
features.description: Stoplight maintains three open-source projects: Spectral (linting), Prism (mocking), and Elements (documentation).

pros: Free perpetual plan available for a single user and project
All paid plans include a 14-day free trial
Visual editor lowers the barrier to working with OpenAPI and JSON Schema
Built-in and shared style guides support API governance across teams
Reusable components reduce duplication across an API portfolio
Open-source tools (Spectral, Prism, Elements) are available alongside the hosted platform
cons: Paid plans charge per additional user beyond the included seats, which can increase cost for larger teams
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published on the site
Free plan is limited to 1 user and 1 project
Now operates as part of SmartBear following its 2023 acquisition, rather than as a fully independent company

faqs.question: Is there a free plan for Stoplight?
faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight offers a Free plan for $0 that includes 1 user, 1 project, the API editor, visual designer, interactive docs, and instant/local mock servers.

faqs.question: Does Stoplight offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans (Basic, Startup, Pro Team) include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Is Stoplight still an active, standalone product?
faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight's website and pricing remain active under the Stoplight brand, though the company now operates as part of SmartBear Software following an acquisition announced in 2023.

faqs.question: What Git providers does Stoplight integrate with?
faqs.answer: Stoplight integrates with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure for Git-based API design workflows.

faqs.question: Does Stoplight provide open-source tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight maintains three open-source projects: Spectral for linting, Prism for mocking, and Elements for documentation.

use_cases.title: Design-first API development
use_cases.description: Developers use the visual editor and mock servers to design, validate, and prototype APIs before writing implementation code.

use_cases.title: API governance across teams
use_cases.description: Shared style guides, component reuse, and activity logs help program leaders enforce consistency across a growing API portfolio.

use_cases.title: Enterprise API strategy
use_cases.description: Enterprise plans offer unlimited projects and teams, custom payment terms, and priority support for organization-wide API transformation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Stoplight?
paragraphs: Stoplight is a design-first OpenAPI platform for creating, documenting, mocking, and managing APIs through a visual editor with comprehensive OpenAPI and JSON Schema support.

The platform includes automatic mock servers, live documentation previews, reusable components, and built-in style guides for design governance, alongside Git sync with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure.
Stoplight was acquired by SmartBear Software, announced in 2023, and now operates as part of SmartBear's API development portfolio while continuing to operate under the Stoplight brand and pricing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Stoplight is for
paragraphs: Stoplight serves developers who want to design quality APIs visually, program leaders who need to coordinate and track API teams, and tech executives driving organization-wide API strategy.

Its free plan suits individual developers working on a single project, while Basic, Startup, and Pro Team plans scale to growing teams, and Enterprise supports large organizations needing unlimited projects, custom terms, and priority support.
